name: Kindergarten Antiques & Material Culture — Semester 2
description: An 18-week, play-based Kindergarten pathway for objects as clues, collections, and respectful stewardship. Lessons are brief, hands-on, flexible, and designed for adult-guided learning.
learningGoals: Build confidence and curiosity with objects as clues, collections, and respectful stewardship.
Communicate learning through talk, drawing, movement, play, or a simple creation.
Practice safe, kind, and responsible participation.

lessons.title: Notice and talk: objects as clues, collections, and respectful stewardship
lessons.objective: I can notice details and share a question about objects as clues, collections, and respectful stewardship through a close look at objects as clues, collections, and respectful stewardship.

lessons.objective: I can notice details and share a question about objects as clues, collections, and respectful stewardship through a guided practice with objects as clues, collections, and respectful stewardship.

lessons.objective: I can notice details and share a question about objects as clues, collections, and respectful stewardship through a small creation or demonstration about objects as clues, collections, and respectful stewardship.

units.title: Wonder and notice: objects as clues, collections, and respectful stewardship
units.essentialQuestion: What can we notice and wonder about objects as clues, collections, and respectful stewardship?
outcomes: I can notice details and share a question about objects as clues, collections, and respectful stewardship.
I can use words, pictures, movement, or materials to communicate my thinking.

activities.title: A close look at objects as clues, collections, and respectful stewardship
activities.learnerAction: With an adult or teacher nearby when needed, the learner participates in a close look at objects as clues, collections, and respectful stewardship, uses words, pictures, movement, or materials to communicate thinking, and cleans up safely.

successCriteria: The learner participates safely and respectfully in objects as clues, collections, and respectful stewardship.
The learner communicates one observation, choice, or question using speech, drawing, movement, or a supported response.
